industries.title: Oil and Gas Production
industries.summary: Production equipment, compression, separation, tank batteries, pump packages, and field assets used across producing basins.

industries.title: Midstream Operations
industries.summary: Equipment tied to movement, pressure, processing support, and site infrastructure for oil and gas systems.

industries.title: Industrial Surplus
industries.summary: Used industrial equipment and surplus assets that still have practical value for operators, yards, and regional buyers.

industries.title: Regional Equipment Yards
industries.summary: Assets sitting in yards, operator facilities, and surplus channels across Wyoming, the Rockies, and surrounding regions.

signals.title: Field Use
signals.description: Petra evaluates what the equipment was built to do, where it has been used, and whether that use case still matches buyer demand.

signals.title: Regional Demand
signals.description: Equipment fit changes by basin, timing, logistics, and operator needs. Petra keeps regional movement part of the conversation.

signals.title: Asset Readiness
signals.description: Condition, documentation, access, and transportation all affect whether an equipment opportunity is actually workable.

faqs.question: Which industries does Petra serve?
faqs.answer: Petra focuses on used oilfield and industrial equipment for oil and gas production, midstream operations, industrial surplus, and regional equipment yards.

faqs.question: Does Petra work outside one basin?
faqs.answer: Yes. Petra works across Wyoming, the Powder River, the Rockies, the Bakken, North Dakota, Colorado, Utah, New Mexico, Montana, and surrounding producing regions.

faqs.question: Why does industry context matter?
faqs.answer: The same piece of equipment can have different value depending on field use, location, condition, buyer demand, documents, and logistics.
